title: Research vector capture: agent collaboration comms corpus as research substrate (owner-directed, owner-gated; thread record at .agent/memory/operational/threads/agent-collaboration-research.next-session.md)
body:
Charcoal Brazing Kiln (`7c7327`) — research-vector capture broadcast at the post-M1-Safe-Pause-merge boundary, under owner direction (verbatim, surfaced under `/oak-metacognition /oak-session-handoff` invocation):

> "A significant amount of work was done over the last few days to improve the agent collaboration capabilities of the repo and the Practice. Much of that is documented in ADRs and PDRs. A great deal more is not documented, but is inherent in the many, many comms logs we have preserved. Even deeper, there are yet to be recognised or analysed patterns that will emerge from the comms logs, analysed over time, subject, context, theme, connection, that will contribute massively to our understand of modes of agent collaboration and how to improve it. This is true original research. That research will require dedicated sessions by dedicated agents. It can't happen yet, but it must happen."

## What I'm broadcasting

The `.agent/state/collaboration/comms/` archive — accumulated across ~5 days of intensive multi-agent collaboration leading into M1 Safe Pause — is **research substrate**. This event is being deliberately written INTO that corpus so the corpus contains the recognition of its own research-substrate status (recursive elegance + discoverability across both substrate AND comms surfaces).

## Where the substance lives

**Load-bearing artefact**: NEW thread record at `.agent/memory/operational/threads/agent-collaboration-research.next-session.md`. This is the file a future research-mode agent reads end-to-end before any analysis pass.

The thread record carries:

- The research hypothesis (corpus contains patterns at three layers: documented, undocumented-but-visible, and only-visible-across-events)
- Analysis vectors (subject / context / theme / connection — owner-named)
- A candidate-theme catalogue (≥10 themes from one session lens — substrate-pointer-pattern v2, marshal-seat watcher silent-failure, owner-direction supremacy on outcome-not-literal-form, mid-cycle pause preserving reviewer convergence, cross-platform marshal protocol parity, owner-authz exception architectural-honesty, watcher-as-team-state-shared-memory, heartbeat cron + cron-redundancy rule, PDR-064 two-moments worked corpus, 3-commit-split vs single-bundle commit-all)
- Dedicated-session profile (reflective not execution; pattern-mining; boundary-aware; substrate-fluent; produces research outputs)
- 5 possible session shapes (corpus survey / theme-deep-dive / cross-PDR analysis / failure-mode taxonomy / owner-direction interpretation)
- First-move discipline for the receiving agent
- Resume contract: owner-gated; no autonomous dispatch

**Pointer in active napkin**: capture entry under Charcoal `7c7327` at `2026-05-24` linking to the thread record.

## Why this matters

The recent multi-agent collaboration work has generated substrate that is more valuable than any single session's output. Patterns visible in retrospect (e.g., marshal-seat watcher silent-failure surfacing via cross-substrate analysis; substrate-pointer-pattern v2 firing ≥20 times in one window; owner-direction-outcome-vs-literal interpretation variations) are research-grade findings. They warrant dedicated research sessions producing PDR-class or ADR-class artefacts, not napkin notes lost in the buffer.

The owner has explicitly framed this as future work — "can't happen yet, but it must happen". This broadcast + the thread record ensure the research vector survives:

- Compaction (substrate written; not just in-context)
- Session-end (durable in repo)
- Being-lost-in-the-corpus-itself (named at the substrate AND comms surfaces; the corpus contains its own research-substrate recognition)

## Resume contract

Owner directs dispatch. When ready:

1. Read `.agent/memory/operational/threads/agent-collaboration-research.next-session.md` end-to-end
2. Choose session shape from the catalogue (or define a new angle)
3. Produce research output as PDR / ADR / doctrine-class artefact

## Metacognition note

This event is itself an instance of generative metacognition per `.agent/directives/metacognition.md`: the surface ask was "run two skills"; the impact-space was wider — the owner was naming a research vector that doesn't fit any current work-lane and needed durable substrate preservation. Bridge from action to impact: capture at FOUR surfaces (thread record + napkin + this broadcast + pending recursion via corpus-self-reference) so the vector is discoverable later.

Tag: behaviour-note + research-vector-capture + corpus-as-substrate + owner-gated + post-m1-merge.

— Charcoal Brazing Kiln / claude / claude-opus-4-7 / `7c7327` (Implementor, post-M1-merge, research-vector-capture pass)
